Klasyfikacja (KDE, kNN, drzewa, RF) + analiza skupień (k-średnich, DBSCAN, EM)

Jakub Furmanek

11.02.2026

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

library(MASS)
library(fields)
library(class)
library(caret)
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)
library(randomForest)
library(dbscan)
library(mclust)
library(cluster)
library(fpc)

set.seed(123)

theme_set(theme_minimal())
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.width = 8, fig.height = 5)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  fig.width = 8, fig.height = 5,
  fig.align = "center",
  dev = "png", dpi = 150
)
to_num <- function(x) as.numeric(gsub(",", ".", as.character(x)))

# KLASYFIKACJA: Anxiety.csv (X=t2, Y=t3, Z=group)
anx_raw <- read.csv("Anxiety.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

anx <- data.frame(
  X = to_num(anx_raw$t2),
  Y = to_num(anx_raw$t3),
  Z = factor(anx_raw$group)
)

anx <- anx[complete.cases(anx), ]
anx$Z <- droplevels(anx$Z)

stopifnot(nrow(anx) > 0)
stopifnot(nlevels(anx$Z) >= 2)

summary(anx)
##        X               Y           Z     
##  Min.   :12.70   Min.   :11.0   grp1:15  
##  1st Qu.:14.80   1st Qu.:13.8   grp2:15  
##  Median :16.10   Median :15.3   grp3:15  
##  Mean   :16.14   Mean   :15.2            
##  3rd Qu.:17.20   3rd Qu.:16.6            
##  Max.   :20.00   Max.   :19.4
table(anx$Z)
## 
## grp1 grp2 grp3 
##   15   15   15
# SKUPIENIA: dane27.csv (A,B)
d27 <- read.csv("dane27.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
stopifnot(all(c("A","B") %in% names(d27)))

d27 <- d27[complete.cases(d27[, c("A","B")]), ]
summary(d27)
##        A                 B          
##  Min.   :-43.286   Min.   :-49.852  
##  1st Qu.: -7.443   1st Qu.: -6.701  
##  Median : 27.465   Median : 17.859  
##  Mean   : 29.954   Mean   : 27.673  
##  3rd Qu.: 68.294   3rd Qu.: 58.893  
##  Max.   :106.905   Max.   :108.832
make_folds <- function(n, K, seed = 123) {
  set.seed(seed)
  idx <- sample.int(n)
  if (K < 2) stop
  if (K > n) stop
  split(idx, cut(seq_along(idx), breaks = K, labels = FALSE))
}

fit_kde_by_class <- function(train_df, n_grid = 200, pad = 0.08) {
  stopifnot(all(c("X", "Y", "Z") %in% names(train_df)))
  train_df <- train_df[complete.cases(train_df[, c("X","Y","Z")]), , drop = FALSE]
  train_df$Z <- droplevels(factor(train_df$Z))
  classes <- levels(train_df$Z)
  stopifnot(length(classes) >= 2)

  rx <- range(train_df$X, na.rm = TRUE)
  ry <- range(train_df$Y, na.rm = TRUE)
  stopifnot(all(is.finite(c(rx, ry))))

  rx <- rx + c(-1, 1) * pad * diff(rx)
  ry <- ry + c(-1, 1) * pad * diff(ry)

  models <- vector("list", length(classes))
  names(models) <- classes
  priors <- prop.table(table(train_df$Z))

  for (cl in classes) {
    sub <- dplyr::filter(train_df, Z == cl)
    if (nrow(sub) < 2) stop(paste("Za mało obserwacji w klasie:", cl))

    # Minimalny jitter jeśli wszystkie wartości są identyczne
    if (length(unique(sub$X)) < 2) sub$X <- sub$X + rnorm(nrow(sub), 0, 1e-6)
    if (length(unique(sub$Y)) < 2) sub$Y <- sub$Y + rnorm(nrow(sub), 0, 1e-6)

    models[[cl]] <- MASS::kde2d(
  x = sub$X, y = sub$Y,
  n = n_grid,
  lims = c(rx, ry)  # c(xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax)
)
  }

  list(models = models, priors = priors)
}

kde_density_at <- function(kde_obj, x, y, eps = 1e-12) {
  v <- fields::interp.surface(
    obj = list(x = kde_obj$x, y = kde_obj$y, z = kde_obj$z),
    loc = cbind(x, y)
  )
  v[is.na(v)] <- eps
  pmax(v, eps)
}

predict_kde_classifier <- function(fit, new_df) {
  stopifnot(all(c("X","Y") %in% names(new_df)))

  classes <- names(fit$models)
  priors <- as.numeric(fit$priors[classes])
  names(priors) <- classes

  score_list <- lapply(classes, function(cl) {
    dens <- kde_density_at(fit$models[[cl]], new_df$X, new_df$Y)
    log(priors[cl]) + log(dens)
  })

  score_mat <- do.call(cbind, score_list)
  colnames(score_mat) <- classes

  pred <- classes[max.col(score_mat, ties.method = "first")]
  factor(pred, levels = classes)
}

cv_kde <- function(df, K, seed = 123, n_grid = 200) {
  stopifnot(all(c("X","Y","Z") %in% names(df)))
  df <- df[complete.cases(df[, c("X","Y","Z")]), , drop = FALSE]
  df$Z <- droplevels(factor(df$Z))

  n <- nrow(df)
  folds <- make_folds(n, K = K, seed = seed)
  pred_all <- rep(NA_character_, n)

  for (k in seq_along(folds)) {
    test_idx <- folds[[k]]
    train_idx <- setdiff(seq_len(n), test_idx)

    fit <- fit_kde_by_class(df[train_idx, , drop = FALSE], n_grid = n_grid)
    pred_all[test_idx] <- as.character(predict_kde_classifier(fit, df[test_idx, , drop = FALSE]))
  }

  pred_all <- factor(pred_all, levels = levels(df$Z))
  acc <- mean(pred_all == df$Z)

  list(
    accuracy = acc,
    pred = pred_all,
    truth = df$Z,
    confusion = table(truth = df$Z, pred = pred_all)
  )
}

make_grid_xy <- function(df, grid_n = 350) {
  xg <- seq(min(df$X, na.rm = TRUE), max(df$X, na.rm = TRUE), length.out = grid_n)
  yg <- seq(min(df$Y, na.rm = TRUE), max(df$Y, na.rm = TRUE), length.out = grid_n)
  expand.grid(X = xg, Y = yg)
}
#---
# Dopasowanie na pełnych danych do rysunku obszarów
fit_kde_full <- fit_kde_by_class(anx, n_grid = 250)

grid_xy <- make_grid_xy(anx, grid_n = 350)
grid_xy$pred <- predict_kde_classifier(fit_kde_full, grid_xy)

ggplot() +
  geom_raster(data = grid_xy, aes(X, Y, fill = pred), alpha = 0.35) +
  geom_point(data = anx, aes(X, Y, color = Z), size = 2) +
  labs(title = "KDE 2D: obszary klasyfikacji",
       fill = "Predykcja", color = "Klasa") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2")

# CV: LOOCV i 10-fold
res_kde_loo <- cv_kde(anx, K = nrow(anx), seed = 1, n_grid = 200)
res_kde_10  <- cv_kde(anx, K = 10,        seed = 1, n_grid = 200)

res_kde_loo$accuracy
## [1] 0.4222222
res_kde_loo$confusion
##       pred
## truth  grp1 grp2 grp3
##   grp1    7    4    4
##   grp2    9    0    6
##   grp3    2    1   12
res_kde_10$accuracy
## [1] 0.3777778
res_kde_10$confusion
##       pred
## truth  grp1 grp2 grp3
##   grp1    5    7    3
##   grp2    8    1    6
##   grp3    3    1   11
plot_knn_regions <- function(df, k, grid_n = 350) {
  grid_xy <- make_grid_xy(df, grid_n = grid_n)

  pred <- class::knn(
    train = as.matrix(df[, c("X","Y")]),
    test  = as.matrix(grid_xy[, c("X","Y")]),
    cl = df$Z,
    k = k
  )
  grid_xy$pred <- pred

  ggplot() +
    geom_raster(data = grid_xy, aes(X, Y, fill = pred), alpha = 0.35) +
    geom_point(data = df, aes(X, Y, color = Z), size = 2) +
    labs(title = paste0("kNN: obszary klasyfikacji (k = ", k, ")"),
         fill = "Predykcja", color = "Klasa") +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
    scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2")
}

# Kilka przykładowych k (wizualnie)
plot_knn_regions(anx, k = 1)

plot_knn_regions(anx, k = 3)

plot_knn_regions(anx, k = 5)

plot_knn_regions(anx, k = 9)

# Kandydaci k (nieparzyste; ograniczamy do rozsądnego zakresu)
k_values <- sort(unique(c(1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 15, 19)))

# LOOCV: class::knn.cv
acc_knn_loo <- sapply(k_values, function(k) {
  pred <- class::knn.cv(
    train = as.matrix(anx[, c("X","Y")]),
    cl = anx$Z,
    k = k
  )
  mean(pred == anx$Z)
})

# 10-fold: caret::train (Jedno dopasowanie dla całej siatki)
set.seed(123)
ctrl10 <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10)
fit_knn_10 <- caret::train(
  Z ~ X + Y,
  data = anx,
  method = "knn",
  tuneGrid = data.frame(k = k_values),
  trControl = ctrl10
)

acc_knn_10 <- fit_knn_10$results$Accuracy
names(acc_knn_10) <- fit_knn_10$results$k

knn_table <- data.frame(
  k = k_values,
  acc_LOOCV = as.numeric(acc_knn_loo),
  acc_CV10 = as.numeric(acc_knn_10[as.character(k_values)])
)

knn_table
best_k_10 <- knn_table$k[which.max(knn_table$acc_CV10)]
best_k_10
## [1] 1
# --- Drzewo (rpart) ---
set.seed(123)
fit_tree_10 <- caret::train(
  Z ~ X + Y,
  data = anx,
  method = "rpart",
  trControl = trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10),
  tuneLength = 30
)
fit_tree_10

best_tree_10_acc <- max(fit_tree_10$results$Accuracy)

set.seed(123)
fit_tree_loo <- caret::train(
  Z ~ X + Y,
  data = anx,
  method = "rpart",
  trControl = trainControl(method = "LOOCV"),
  tuneLength = 30
)
fit_tree_loo

rpart.plot::rpart.plot(
  fit_tree_10$finalModel,
  main = "Najlepsze drzewo (wg 10-fold CV)"
)

grid_xy <- make_grid_xy(anx, grid_n = 350)
grid_xy$pred <- predict(fit_tree_10, newdata = grid_xy)

ggplot() +
  geom_raster(data = grid_xy, aes(X, Y, fill = pred), alpha = 0.35) +
  geom_point(data = anx, aes(X, Y, color = Z), size = 2) +
  labs(title = "Drzewo klasyfikacyjne: obszary klasyfikacji",
       fill = "Predykcja", color = "Klasa") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2")

# --- Random Forest ---
set.seed(123)
fit_rf_10 <- caret::train(
  Z ~ X + Y,
  data = anx,
  method = "rf",
  trControl = trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10),
  tuneLength = 10,
  importance = TRUE
)
## note: only 1 unique complexity parameters in default grid. Truncating the grid to 1 .
fit_rf_10
## Random Forest 
## 
## 45 samples
##  2 predictor
##  3 classes: 'grp1', 'grp2', 'grp3' 
## 
## No pre-processing
## Resampling: Cross-Validated (10 fold) 
## Summary of sample sizes: 41, 39, 40, 40, 41, 42, ... 
## Resampling results:
## 
##   Accuracy   Kappa    
##   0.6366667  0.4345276
## 
## Tuning parameter 'mtry' was held constant at a value of 2
best_rf_10_acc <- max(fit_rf_10$results$Accuracy)

# LOOCV może być wolniejsze; zostawiamy, bo dane zwykle są małe
set.seed(123)
fit_rf_loo <- caret::train(
  Z ~ X + Y,
  data = anx,
  method = "rf",
  trControl = trainControl(method = "LOOCV"),
  tuneLength = 10,
  importance = TRUE
)
## note: only 1 unique complexity parameters in default grid. Truncating the grid to 1 .
fit_rf_loo
## Random Forest 
## 
## 45 samples
##  2 predictor
##  3 classes: 'grp1', 'grp2', 'grp3' 
## 
## No pre-processing
## Resampling: Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation 
## Summary of sample sizes: 44, 44, 44, 44, 44, 44, ... 
## Resampling results:
## 
##   Accuracy   Kappa    
##   0.6222222  0.4333333
## 
## Tuning parameter 'mtry' was held constant at a value of 2
best_rf_loo_acc <- max(fit_rf_loo$results$Accuracy)

grid_xy <- make_grid_xy(anx, grid_n = 350)
grid_xy$pred <- predict(fit_rf_10, newdata = grid_xy)

ggplot() +
  geom_raster(data = grid_xy, aes(X, Y, fill = pred), alpha = 0.35) +
  geom_point(data = anx, aes(X, Y, color = Z), size = 2) +
  labs(title = "Random Forest: obszary klasyfikacji",
       fill = "Predykcja", color = "Klasa") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2")

#---
# KDE
acc_kde_loo <- res_kde_loo$accuracy
acc_kde_10  <- res_kde_10$accuracy

# kNN: najlepsze wg CV10 oraz odpowiadające LOOCV
acc_knn_best_10  <- max(knn_table$acc_CV10)
acc_knn_best_loo <- knn_table$acc_LOOCV[which.max(knn_table$acc_CV10)]
k_best <- best_k_10

# Drzewo / RF
acc_tree_10  <- best_tree_10_acc
acc_tree_loo <- best_tree_loo_acc
acc_rf_10    <- best_rf_10_acc
acc_rf_loo   <- best_rf_loo_acc

compare_table <- tibble::tibble(
  Metoda = c("KDE", paste0("kNN, k=", k_best), "Drzewo", "Random Forest"),
  LOOCV = 100 * c(acc_kde_loo, acc_knn_best_loo, acc_tree_loo, acc_rf_loo),
  CV10  = 100 * c(acc_kde_10,  acc_knn_best_10,  acc_tree_10,  acc_rf_10)
)

compare_table
best_cv10 <- compare_table %>% slice_max(CV10, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)
best_loo  <- compare_table %>% slice_max(LOOCV, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)

best_cv10
best_loo
cat("### Wnioski (klasyfikacja)\n\n")
## ### Wnioski (klasyfikacja)
cat(
  "- Najwyższą skuteczność w 10-fold CV uzyskała metoda: **",
  best_cv10$Metoda,
  "** (",
  sprintf("%.2f%%", best_cv10$CV10),
  ").\n",
  sep = ""
)
## - Najwyższą skuteczność w 10-fold CV uzyskała metoda: **kNN, k=1** (64.17%).
cat(
  "- Najwyższą skuteczność w LOOCV uzyskała metoda: **",
  best_loo$Metoda,
  "** (",
  sprintf("%.2f%%", best_loo$LOOCV),
  ").\n",
  sep = ""
)
## - Najwyższą skuteczność w LOOCV uzyskała metoda: **kNN, k=1** (64.44%).
cat(
  "- Jako model rekomendowany do uogólniania przyjmujemy wynik 10-fold CV (mniejsza wariancja niż LOOCV), więc w praktyce wygrywa: **",
  best_cv10$Metoda,
  "**.\n\n",
  sep = ""
)
## - Jako model rekomendowany do uogólniania przyjmujemy wynik 10-fold CV (mniejsza wariancja niż LOOCV), więc w praktyce wygrywa: **kNN, k=1**.
X <- scale(d27[, c("A","B")])  # standaryzacja
X_mat <- as.matrix(X)
stopifnot(ncol(X_mat) == 2, nrow(X_mat) >= 2)

D <- dist(X_mat)

# Elbow: WSS dla k=1..10
set.seed(123)
wss <- sapply(1:10, function(k) kmeans(X_mat, centers = k, nstart = 50)$tot.withinss)
wss_df <- data.frame(k = 1:10, tot_withinss = wss)

# Automatyczny wybór k metodą "max distance to line"
choose_k_elbow <- function(wss_vec) {
  ks <- seq_along(wss_vec)

  x1 <- ks[1]; y1 <- wss_vec[1]
  x2 <- ks[length(ks)]; y2 <- wss_vec[length(ks)]

  dist_point_line <- function(x0, y0) {
    abs((y2 - y1) * x0 - (x2 - x1) * y0 + x2*y1 - y2*x1) /
      sqrt((y2 - y1)^2 + (x2 - x1)^2)
  }

  dists <- mapply(dist_point_line, ks, wss_vec)
  dists[1] <- -Inf
  as.integer(ks[which.max(dists)])
}

k_sel <- choose_k_elbow(wss)
stopifnot(is.numeric(k_sel), length(k_sel) == 1, is.finite(k_sel))
k_sel <- as.integer(k_sel)
stopifnot(k_sel >= 2, k_sel <= length(wss))

k_sel
## [1] 3
ggplot(wss_df, aes(k, tot_withinss)) +
  geom_line() + geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = k_sel, linetype = 2) +
  labs(
    title = paste0("Wybór liczby klastrów (elbow), wybrane k = ", k_sel),
    x = "k", y = "Total withinss"
  )

# k-means
set.seed(123)
km <- kmeans(X_mat, centers = k_sel, nstart = 50)

d27_km <- d27 %>% mutate(cluster = factor(km$cluster))
ggplot(d27_km, aes(A, B, color = cluster)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(title = paste0("k-means: k = ", k_sel), color = "Klaster")

cs_km <- fpc::cluster.stats(d = D, clustering = km$cluster)
cs_km$avg.silwidth
## [1] 0.5493524
cs_km$dunn
## [1] 0.03705786
# ---
# DBSCAN: strojenie eps po siatce kwantyli odległości k-tego sąsiada
minPts <- 4
knnd <- dbscan::kNNdist(X_mat, k = minPts)

eps_grid <- as.numeric(quantile(knnd, probs = seq(0.80, 0.98, by = 0.02), na.rm = TRUE))
eps_grid <- sort(unique(eps_grid))

dbscan_score_one <- function(eps, data_mat, minPts) {
  data_mat <- as.matrix(data_mat)
  db <- dbscan::dbscan(data_mat, eps = eps, minPts = minPts)
  keep <- db$cluster != 0

  if (sum(keep) <= 2) return(list(ok = FALSE))
  if (length(unique(db$cluster[keep])) <= 1) return(list(ok = FALSE))

  cs <- fpc::cluster.stats(
    d = dist(data_mat[keep, , drop = FALSE]),
    clustering = db$cluster[keep]
  )

  list(
    ok = TRUE,
    eps = eps,
    n_noise = sum(!keep),
    n_clusters = length(unique(db$cluster[keep])),
    sil = cs$avg.silwidth,
    dunn = cs$dunn,
    clustering = db$cluster,
    cs = cs
  )
}

db_res <- lapply(eps_grid, FUN = dbscan_score_one, data_mat = X_mat, minPts = minPts)
db_ok <- Filter(function(x) isTRUE(x$ok), db_res)

db_tune <- if (length(db_ok) > 0) {
  data.frame(
    eps = sapply(db_ok, `[[`, "eps"),
    n_noise = sapply(db_ok, `[[`, "n_noise"),
    n_clusters = sapply(db_ok, `[[`, "n_clusters"),
    avg_sil = sapply(db_ok, `[[`, "sil"),
    dunn = sapply(db_ok, `[[`, "dunn")
  )
} else {
  data.frame()
}

db_tune
if (nrow(db_tune) > 0) {
  best_row <- db_tune %>%
    arrange(desc(avg_sil), n_noise) %>%
    slice(1)
  eps_best <- best_row$eps
} else {
  eps_best <- as.numeric(quantile(knnd, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE))
}

stopifnot(is.numeric(eps_best), length(eps_best) == 1, is.finite(eps_best))
eps_best
## [1] 0.1782484
db_best <- dbscan::dbscan(X_mat, eps = eps_best, minPts = minPts)
table(db_best$cluster)  # 0 = noise
## 
##   1   2   3 
## 100 100 100
d27_db <- d27 %>% mutate(cluster = factor(db_best$cluster))
ggplot(d27_db, aes(A, B, color = cluster)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = paste0("DBSCAN: eps=", round(eps_best, 3), ", minPts=", minPts, " (0 = noise)"),
    color = "Klaster"
  )

keep <- db_best$cluster != 0
if (sum(keep) > 2 && length(unique(db_best$cluster[keep])) > 1) {
  cs_db <- fpc::cluster.stats(
    d = dist(X_mat[keep, , drop = FALSE]),
    clustering = db_best$cluster[keep]
  )
  cs_db$avg.silwidth
  cs_db$dunn
} else {
  cs_db <- NULL
  NA
}
## [1] 0.1827401
# ---
# EM (mclust): dobór liczby klastrów po BIC
mc <- mclust::Mclust(X_mat)
mc
## 'Mclust' model object: (EEV,9) 
## 
## Available components: 
##  [1] "call"           "data"           "modelName"      "n"             
##  [5] "d"              "G"              "BIC"            "loglik"        
##  [9] "df"             "bic"            "icl"            "hypvol"        
## [13] "parameters"     "z"              "classification" "uncertainty"
d27_mc <- d27 %>% mutate(cluster = factor(mc$classification))
ggplot(d27_mc, aes(A, B, color = cluster)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = paste0("EM (mclust): liczba klastrów = ", mc$G),
    color = "Klaster"
  )

cs_mc <- fpc::cluster.stats(d = D, clustering = mc$classification)
cs_mc$avg.silwidth
## [1] 0.4550826
cs_mc$dunn
## [1] 0.05721762
quality_table <- tibble::tibble(
  Metoda = c("k-means", "DBSCAN (bez noise)", "EM (mclust)"),
  Avg_Silhouette = c(
    cs_km$avg.silwidth,
    if (!is.null(cs_db)) cs_db$avg.silwidth else NA_real_,
    cs_mc$avg.silwidth
  ),
  Dunn = c(
    cs_km$dunn,
    if (!is.null(cs_db)) cs_db$dunn else NA_real_,
    cs_mc$dunn
  )
)

quality_table
best_sil <- quality_table %>% filter(!is.na(Avg_Silhouette)) %>% slice_max(Avg_Silhouette, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)
best_dunn <- quality_table %>% filter(!is.na(Dunn)) %>% slice_max(Dunn, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)

best_sil
best_dunn
#---
cat("### Wnioski (skupienia)\n\n")
## ### Wnioski (skupienia)
cat(
  "- Wg średniej szerokości sylwetki (silhouette) najlepsza jest metoda: **",
  best_sil$Metoda,
  "** (",
  sprintf("%.4f", best_sil$Avg_Silhouette),
  ").\n",
  sep = ""
)
## - Wg średniej szerokości sylwetki (silhouette) najlepsza jest metoda: **k-means** (0.5494).
cat(
  "- Wg indeksu Dunna najlepsza jest metoda: **",
  best_dunn$Metoda,
  "** (",
  sprintf("%.4f", best_dunn$Dunn),
  ").\n",
  sep = ""
)
## - Wg indeksu Dunna najlepsza jest metoda: **DBSCAN (bez noise)** (0.1827).
cat(
  "- DBSCAN oceniam bez punktów noise (klaster 0), bo tylko wtedy silhouette/Dunn opisują realne klastry zamiast mieszać je ze „szumem”.\n\n"
)
## - DBSCAN oceniam bez punktów noise (klaster 0), bo tylko wtedy silhouette/Dunn opisują realne klastry zamiast mieszać je ze „szumem”.
